frameworks.name: prediction error
frameworks.one_line: encodes mismatch between prior expectation and sensory input

core_question: Do pupil dilation and the slow-wave ERP track surprise driven by intrinsic variability in decision confidence rather than external task variables?
key_assumptions: expectation of being correct fluctuates due to internal state (e.g., engagement)
pupil and slow-wave ERP index rapid changes in brain arousal
surprise = mismatch between expected correctness and feedback outcome
manipulations: feedback outcome (correct vs error) on each trial
trial-by-trial intrinsic variability in decision confidence
key_findings: both pupil response and slow-wave ERP reflect surprise about choice outcome
pupil response and slow-wave ERP are unrelated to each other
prediction-error computations depend on feedback awareness
interpretations: phasic arousal markers index a learning-relevant surprise signal driven by internal confidence
pupil and slow-wave ERP capture dissociable arousal-linked components of feedback processing
